title: Rauthy config.toml (subset)
description: Local subset schema for Rauthy's config.toml. Upstream Rauthy does NOT publish a JSON schema for this file, so this only covers the sections we actively customize. Other top-level keys/tables pass through (additionalProperties=true at the root). Adding type/value constraints here protects the high-risk surface where upstream sample values silently override our env-var inputs — see rauthy-config.toml comments and feedback_verify_shipped_config_defaults.md.

bootstrap.description: First-boot setup of the default admin user, OIDC client, and bootstrap dir. The full upstream field list (per src/data/src/rauthy_config.rs VarsBootstrap) is mirrored in `properties` below; `additionalProperties: false` catches typos like `admin_emails` instead of `admin_email`. Per the typo upstream uses, the argon2 key is `pasword_argon2id` (one `s`) — DO NOT rename.

admin_email.description: Default `admin@localhost`. Overridden by BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_EMAIL. If you change this AND insert the same email via bootstrap users.json, the users.json INSERT silently fails on unique-constraint, and the env-var password wins.

password_plain.description: Upstream ships a sample value here. Set BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD_PLAIN env var instead — the env override maps to THIS field, NOT to `pasword_argon2id`.

pasword_argon2id.description: Note the typo `pasword` (missing second `s`) — this is the actual upstream key, do not correct it. Upstream ships a real sample hash that takes precedence over `password_plain` AND its env override. Leaving the sample uncommented makes BOOTSTRAP_ADMIN_PASSWORD_PLAIN silently ignored. The `not.const` guard fails the lint if anyone re-adds the exact upstream sample hash.

bootstrap_dir.description: Filesystem directory containing groups.json/users.json/clients.json. Overridden by BOOTSTRAP_DIR. We mount /app/bootstrap from a ConfigMap (see rauthy.ts).
